oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_parse_mount_configuration.json.description: Parses mount request parameters into normalized connection, pod, permission, and secret descriptor fields.

feature2_configuration_validation_errors.description: Rejects mount configurations that are missing required fields or contain ambiguous duplicate output object names.

feature3_secret_api_request_rendering.js.description: Builds a secret retrieval HTTP request from a requested secret path, optional query string, optional method, and optional JSON body arguments.

cases.expected_output:
method=GET
path=/v1/secret/foo
query=
body=
file_path=requested-object
file_mode=420
file_contents=ok

method=GET
path=/v1/secret/foo
query=
body=
file_path=requested-object
file_mode=420
file_contents=ok

feature4_secret_value_extraction.json.description: Extracts one named value from a secret response, unwrapping embedded data objects and rendering strings plainly while rendering structured values as compact JSON.

feature5_mount_response_and_cache_behavi.description: Mounts multiple secret descriptors through mocked external APIs and returns files, permissions, object versions, and observable API request traffic including cache reuse.

feature6_connection_settings_precedence..description: Resolves connection settings from environment, runtime flags, and mount parameters using the documented precedence and accepts certificate trust material supplied as either file or directory.

feature7_version_metadata_rendering.json.description: Renders provider version metadata as a single compact JSON line containing binary version, build date, runtime version, and supported driver floor.

_constraints.output_rule: Output must be lines of 'key=value' format representing normalized configuration.
